title: Sub-workflow execution failure due to missing trigger node
symptom: The sub-workflow fails to execute with the error 'Missing node to start execution'. This occurs even though the workflow contains a valid Execute Workflow Trigger node.
applies_when: Using Execute Workflow node in parent workflow
Sub-workflow contains an Execute Workflow Trigger
not_this_if: Sub-workflow is correctly configured with a valid trigger node
root_cause: The error occurs when the parent workflow attempts to call a sub-workflow that does not have a properly configured Execute Workflow Trigger node. This can happen if there are additional nodes that interfere with the execution flow or if the workflow structure is not recognized correctly by n8n.
fix_steps: Open the sub-workflow in the editor
Remove any unnecessary final nodes that may disrupt execution
Ensure the Execute Workflow Trigger is the last node in the workflow
Save the workflow and try executing the parent workflow again
agent_instruction: Remove any final nodes in the sub-workflow that may interfere with execution.
